turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Julie Szo opened Green Tree Lawn Care Company on April 1, 2021. At April 30, 2021, the trial balance is as follows: green tree lawn care company trial balance april 30,2021 cash debit $10950 credit_, prepaid insurance debit 3600 credit_, supplies debit 850 credit_, equipment debit 28000 credit_, notes payable debit_, credit 20000, account payable debit_, credit 450_, unearned revenue debit_, credit 4200, j.szo capital debit_, credit 18000, j.szo, drawings debit 650, credit_, service revenue debit_, credit 1800, rent expense debit 400, credit_, totals debit $44450, credit $44450 . Required:
a. Prepare the adjusting entries for the month of April. Show calculations.
b. Prepare accounts for the accounts affected by the adjusting entries. Post the adjusting entries to the T accounts.
c. Prepare an adjusted trial balance at April 30, 2021,
d. Prepare an income statement, statement of owner's equity, and balance sheet.Analysis reveals the following additional data for the month:
$20,000
450
1. Prepaid insurance is the cost of a 12-month insurance policy that started April 1.
2. Supplies costing $225 were on hand on April 30.
3. The equipment is expected to have a useful life of four years.
4. The note payable is dated April 1. It is a six-month, 4% note with interest payable on the first of each month starting on May 1.
5. Seven customers paid for the company's six-month lawn service package of $600, beginning in April. These customers were served in April.
6. Lawn services performed for other customers but not billed or recorded at April
30 totaled $1,500.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:

1. Insurance Expense (($3,600/12) x 1) = $300

2. Supplies Expense      $425

3. Depreciation Expense     ($28,000 / 4 / 12) = $583.33 (rounded to nearest cent)

4. Interest Expense       ($20,000 x 4% x 1/12) = $66.67
     Interest Payable                $66.67
5. Unearned Revenue     ($600 x 6 x 7/12) = $2,100
     Service Revenue                  $2,100
6. Service Revenue           $1,500
